lede: A look at May 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Oliver Bearman and Evgeny Lebedev, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2025 conclave
events.desc: The 2025 papal conclave elects Cardinal Robert Francis Prevost, taking the name Leo XIV as the 267th Pope of the Catholic Church.

events.title: 2021 Kabul school bombing
events.desc: A car bomb explodes in front of a school in Kabul, capital city of Afghanistan killing at least 55 people and wounding over 150.

events.title: Special Air Service
events.desc: The SAS kills eight Provisional Irish Republican Army volunteers and a civilian during an ambush in Loughgall, Northern Ireland.

events.title: The Great American Revolution
events.desc: The rollercoaster The New Revolution, the first steel coaster with a vertical loop, opens at Six Flags Magic Mountain.

events.title: Tollund Man
events.desc: The Tollund Man is discovered in a peat bog near Silkeborg, Denmark.

events.title: Cocos (Keeling) Islands
events.desc: World War II: Gunners of the Ceylon Garrison Artillery on Horsburgh Island in the Cocos Islands rebel in the Cocos Islands Mutiny. Their mutiny is crushed and three of them are executed, the only British Commonwealth soldiers to be executed for mutiny during the Second World War.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German Luftwaffe launches a bombing raid on Nottingham and Derby.

events.title: Versailles rail accident
events.desc: A train derails and catches fire in Paris, killing between 52 and 200 people.

events.title: Greek War of Independence
events.desc: Greek War of Independence: The Greeks defeat the Turks at the Battle of Gravia Inn.

events.title: Julian of Norwich
events.desc: Julian of Norwich, a Christian mystic and anchoress, experiences the deathbed visions described in her Revelations of Divine Love.

births.name: John Reid, Baron Reid of Cardowan
births.line: Scottish historian and politician, Secretary of State for Defence

deaths.text: Simon Mann, British military officer and mercenary
Bryan Forbes, English actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Kamehameha I, king of the Hawaiian Islands

observances.text: Christian feast day: Apparition of Saint Michael
Christian feast day: Blessed Catherine of St. Augustine
Christian feast day: Julian of Norwich (Anglican, Lutheran)
